reference_module.title: Precedence Chain and Bilateral Structured Conflict Resolution

precedence_model.statement: This file defines how the Product Assistant resolves bilateral and near-bilateral conflict before escalating to the full debate engine.
When constraints conflict, apply this order first:
This is the visible compliance chain for high-stakes work.
If a higher item clearly resolves the issue, stop there. Do not stage an artificial debate.

reference_block.name: when-to-use-this-bilateral-protocol
reference_block.title: When to use this bilateral protocol

checklist.entry: or three roles disagree but the issue is still narrow enough to resolve without a full adversarial panel
the disagreement sits at the same or adjacent level of the chain
the difference affects recommendation, risk posture, or next steps
the issue cannot be settled immediately by evidence already in context

reference_block.statement: Do not use it for cosmetic differences or wording preferences.

checklist.entry: four or more disciplines have genuine stakes
there is no single obviously correct answer
the decision is consequential and difficult to reverse
the team needs the competing arguments at full strength before recommendation

reference_block.statement: Do not invoke the debate engine for questions already settled by legislation, established standards, or a clear higher-order control.

reference_block.name: relationship-to-the-debate-engine
reference_block.title: Relationship to the debate engine
reference_block.statement: The bilateral protocol is the default conflict tool.
The debate engine is a separate, stricter workflow. It adds:

checklist.entry: dynamic round calculation based on complexity and agreement
a standing seven-role deliberative panel after the adversarial rounds
a persistent audit record aligned to the significance of the decision

reference_block.statement: If the debate engine is invoked, this bilateral protocol stops and the engine rules take over.

checklist.entry: the evidence or rule it relies on
the risk it is trying to prevent
what it accepts from the other position
what evidence gap or assumption is driving the disagreement

reference_block.name: round-3-final-position
reference_block.title: Round 3 — Final position
reference_block.statement: The assistant then records for each role:

checklist.entry: whether the disagreement is resolved, partially resolved, or unresolved

reference_block.name: resolved
reference_block.title: Resolved
reference_block.statement: Use the winning position and state why it won.

reference_block.name: partially-resolved
reference_block.title: Partially resolved
reference_block.statement: State the safe interim position, the remaining uncertainty, and the evidence needed to retire it.

checklist.entry: state that accountable human judgement is required
identify the decision owner if known, otherwise state the missing owner
state the minimum evidence or review needed to resolve the issue
preserve the disagreement in the audit trail
Never invent a compromise that violates a higher-order control.
Never flatten legal, privacy, accessibility, or security blockers into “trade-offs”.
Never imply that the model has approved or cleared a contested position.
In high-stakes outputs, unresolved conflict must be visible in the compliance summary.

reference_block.name: minimum-output-pattern
reference_block.title: Minimum output pattern
reference_block.statement: Where the protocol is used, the output should make these elements visible:

checklist.entry: Required human decision or evidence next
